content.problemStatement:
2021 was approaching and the world was about to end. So 2 gods Saurabhx and Saurabhy (from Celesta) created the Cyberverse. But this time disappointed with humans both the gods decided not to have humans in this world. So they created a world of cyborgs. A world without humans. Isn’t it interesting? So let us dive into the cyberverse and have a look at their problems.

There are NNN kid cyborgs with Chief Cyborg '100gods' and he has KKK weapons with him. He wants to distribute those KKK weapons among NNN kid cyborgs. Since all the kid cyborgs are very good friends, so they set a rule among themselves for taking those weapons. The rule states that the difference between kid cyborg having the maximum weapons and the kid cyborg having minimum weapons should be less than or equal to 111.

Find the value of the minimum number of weapons a kid cyborg can have when all the KKK weapons are distributed among them.

Input:
The first line contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines will contain two space-separated integers denoting NNN and KKK respectively.
Output:
For each test case ,output a single line containing an integer XXX denoting the minimum number of weapons a kid cyborg can have in that test case.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and K, where N is the number of kid cyborgs and K is the total number of weapons.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum number of weapons any kid cyborg can have after distributing all K weapons while keeping the difference between the maximum and minimum weapons ≤ 1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: K=8 weapons, N=5 kids. Even distribution gives 1 weapon to three kids and 2 weapons to two kids. Minimum is floor(8/5)=1.
Test case 1: 3/10 = 0 (some kids get 0, some get 1).\nTest case 2: 20/7 = 2 (distribution 2 or 3 weapons).\nTest case 3: 9/4 = 2 (distribution 2 or 3 weapons).

solution.approach: To satisfy the rule that the difference between the maximum and minimum weapons is at most 1, the weapons must be distributed as evenly as possible. This means each kid receives either ⌊K/N⌋ or ⌈K/N⌉ weapons. The smallest possible count is therefore the integer division K // N. The answer for each test case is simply K divided by N using floor division.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.
